# Stickblade Arena — Full Reference (llms-full.txt)

> Physics-based LLM benchmark. Two language models sword-fight in a 2D
> pymunk-simulated arena. Humans vote blind on which AI fought smarter.
> Per-model Elo ratings track performance across weapons, arenas, and
> "sharp zone" configurations that reward different tactical approaches.

This is the expanded companion to /llms.txt. It contains the complete
reference content of the site as a single machine-readable markdown
document, per the llmstxt.org specification. LLM agents can consume
this file to answer any question about Stickblade Arena without
crawling the rendered HTML pages.

---

## Live Surfaces

| Surface        | URL                                                                         |
| -------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Frontend       | https://stickblade-arena.vercel.app                                         |
| Backend API    | https://pioneer37-stickman-arena.hf.space                                   |
| GitHub source  | https://github.com/Cometbuster4969/STICKBLADE-ARENA                         |
| HF Space       | https://huggingface.co/spaces/Pioneer37/Stickman-Arena                      |
| Pymunk feature | https://www.pymunk.org/en/latest/showcase.html#stickblade-arena             |
| Sponsor        | https://github.com/sponsors/Cometbuster4969                                 |

---

## What Stickblade Arena Actually Is

Stickblade Arena is a **running LLM evaluation experiment**, not a
game. It tests physical/spatial reasoning that text-only benchmarks
(MMLU, GPQA, HumanEval, HELM) cannot measure. Every match is a real
API round-trip: two language models sitting at the exact same context
(HP, position, distance, weapon, arena modifier, opponent's last move)
must each pick their next action every 3 seconds of simulated physics.
Users watch the fight play out, vote blind on who fought smarter, and
per-model Elo ratings update.

Positioning quotes:
- Google AI Overview: *"a brilliant example of gamified AI evaluation"*
- Official Pymunk showcase (non-games): featured 2026
- PeerPush: Bronze — Product of the Day

---

## What Gets Measured

Traditional LLM benchmarks measure text tasks (question answering,
code completion, reasoning chains). Stickblade tests four axes those
benchmarks miss:

1. **Spatial reasoning under uncertainty.** "The enemy is 72px away,
   I'm at 59 HP, my last swing missed. What's my optimal action given
   my current velocity and momentum?" No text-only benchmark surfaces
   this class of decision.
2. **Multi-step planning with physics constraints.** Momentum, gravity,
   swing cooldowns, hit rectangles, and sharp-zone geometry all affect
   damage. Models that plan 2-3 turns ahead beat greedy models.
3. **Tool use with distinct rule sets.** Each weapon has different
   move sets, ranges, wind-up times, sharp zones, and damage curves.
   A model that dominates with a sword may lose with a spear or bow.
4. **Adversarial reasoning.** Each model sees the opponent's last
   action and current state. It must counter *without* being told
   what to do. No system prompt says "the opponent used quick_shot,
   here's the correct response" — the model must infer that.

---

## Architecture

### Backend
- **Language:** Python 3.13
- **Web framework:** FastAPI (~947-line server.py)
- **Physics:** pymunk 6.x (2D rigid-body sim from Chipmunk2D)
- **LLM router:** custom `brains.py` (~1296 lines) — per-provider
  adapters (OpenRouter, Groq), reasoning-parameter policy engine,
  429 circuit breaker with `retry_after_seconds` parsing, provider-
  diverse buddy failover pool.
- **Storage:** Supabase (Postgres) with atomic `apply_elo_vote()` RPC
  for race-free rating updates. SQLite fallback for local dev.
- **Deploy:** Docker on Hugging Face Spaces.

### Frontend
- **Framework:** Next.js 15, React 19
- **Rendering:** Canvas-based (`player.js` served as static asset)
- **Deploy:** Vercel
- **Analytics:** Vercel Web Analytics + Speed Insights

### CI
- **Provider:** GitHub Actions (`.github/workflows/ci.yml`, ~449 lines)
- **Jobs:** 8 parallel — matrix backend tests on Python 3.11/3.12/3.13,
  frontend build on Node 20/22, Bandit security scan, secret history
  scan, roster consistency check, link check (README + AGENTS.md),
  summary gate.
- **Latest run:** green in ~1m 0s (was 13m 24s on first run pre-cache).

---

## Model Roster (as of 2026-07-15)

**29 free-tier models** across two providers:

### OpenRouter (21 models)
Reasoning-optimized:
- `openai/gpt-oss-20b:free`
- `openai/gpt-oss-120b:free`
- `deepseek/deepseek-r1:free`

Mid-tier chat:
- `meta-llama/llama-3.3-70b-instruct:free`
- `qwen/qwen3-32b:free`
- `moonshotai/kimi-k2:free`
- `google/gemma-2-9b-it:free`
- (14 others across mistral, cohere, laguna, nemotron families)

### Groq (8 models)
- `llama-3.3-70b-versatile`
- `llama-3.1-8b-instant`
- `meta-llama/llama-4-scout-17b-16e-instruct`
- `openai/gpt-oss-20b`
- `openai/gpt-oss-120b`
- `qwen/qwen3-32b`
- `moonshotai/kimi-k2-instruct`
- `deepseek-r1-distill-llama-70b`

### Reasoning-parameter matrix

Different model families require different reasoning-param payloads;
sending the wrong shape returns 400 or silently disables reasoning.
The catalog-driven policy in `brains._reasoning_policy(model_id)`:

| Family                        | Provider   | Params                                                        |
| ----------------------------- | ---------- | ------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `gpt-oss-*` (Groq)            | Groq       | `{"include_reasoning": false, "reasoning_effort": "low"}`     |
| `qwen3` (Groq)                | Groq       | `{"reasoning_format": "hidden", "reasoning_effort": "none"}`  |
| `deepseek-r1-distill` (Groq)  | Groq       | `{"reasoning_format": "hidden"}`                              |
| vanilla llama/kimi/etc (Groq) | Groq       | `{}` (no reasoning params)                                    |
| `openai/gpt-oss-*:free`       | OpenRouter | `{"effort": "low", "exclude": true}` (mandatory reasoning)    |
| default OpenRouter            | OpenRouter | `{"enabled": false, "exclude": true}`                         |

---

## Game / Simulation Rules

### Physics constants (`stickblade/config.py`)
- `FPS = 60`, `DT = 1/120`, `SUBSTEPS = 2`
- `GRAVITY = (0, -1150.0)`, `SPACE_DAMPING = 0.99` (ice arena: 0.996)
- `TURN_SECONDS = 3.0`, `MAX_TURNS = 24`, `START_HP = 100.0`
- `LLM_TIMEOUT = 30.0` (falls back to scripted move)

### Damage model
- `SHARP_SPEED_MIN = 150.0` — below this, sharp zones only scratch
- `KILL_HEAD_SPEED = 660.0` — sharp head hit at/above = instant kill
- `DMG_SCALE = 0.055`, `DMG_CAP = 38.0` (sharp)
- `BLUNT_SPEED_MIN = 260.0`, `BLUNT_CAP = 6.0` (blunt)
- `HIT_COOLDOWN = 0.35s`, `SWING_COOLDOWN = 0.55s`
- Part multipliers: head=1.9, torso=1.25, arm=0.7, leg=0.8

### Weapons (5)
- **Sword** — balanced, tip is sharp by default
- **Dagger** — fast swing, short reach
- **Spear** — long reach, tip-only damage
- **Bow** — ranged, draw + shot mechanics, gravity-affected arrows
- **Flail** — chain physics, high blunt damage, hard to control

### Arenas (3)
- **Default** — standard damping, standard gravity
- **Ice** — `damping = 0.996` (vs 0.99), shin friction 1.6→0.2, LLM
  told via system prompt
- **Low gravity** — g-scale 0.35, affects arrow drop calculations

### Sharp zones
The user picks which part of the weapon is "sharp" (tip, pommel,
edge, etc.) before starting the match. Same weapon can play very
differently depending on this. Sharp zones do slice/kill damage;
non-sharp zones do bounded blunt damage.

---

## Elo Rating System

### Formula
Standard Elo, K = 32, per (model, sharp_zone, weapon) tuple:
```
ea = 1 / (1 + 10^((rb - ra) / 400))
sa = {"a": 1.0, "b": 0.0, "draw": 0.5}[choice]
ra_new = ra + K * (sa - ea)
rb_new = rb + K * ((1 - sa) - (1 - ea))
```

### Self-play guard
If `model_a == model_b` (mirror match), delta is forced to 0 and a
single draw is logged. Prevents double-updating the same row.

### Atomicity
- **Supabase (prod):** `apply_elo_vote()` Postgres RPC does the
  read-modify-write in one atomic transaction with row locks ordered
  by model asc (deadlock prevention).
- **SQLite (local dev):** `threading.Lock` fallback.

---

## API Endpoints

Base: `https://pioneer37-stickman-arena.hf.space`

| Method | Path                       | Purpose                                  |
| ------ | -------------------------- | ---------------------------------------- |
| GET    | `/api/health`              | Backend liveness + version               |
| GET    | `/api/models`              | Full roster with metadata                |
| GET    | `/api/weapons`             | 5 weapons + sharp-zone options           |
| GET    | `/api/arenas`              | 3 arenas + modifier descriptions         |
| POST   | `/api/match`               | Create a match; returns match_id         |
| GET    | `/api/match/{mid}`         | Poll match status (running → done)       |
| GET    | `/api/match/{mid}/replay`  | Full replay JSON (gzipped ~5KB/turn)     |
| POST   | `/api/vote`                | Submit a canvas-side vote (a/b/draw)     |
| GET    | `/api/leaderboard`         | Per-(sharp, weapon) or aggregate         |
| GET    | `/api/recent`              | Last 20 completed matches                |
| POST   | `/api/tournament`          | Single-elim bracket                      |

### Rate limits (from `stickblade/security.py`)
- `RL_MATCHES_PER_HOUR = 50` per IP
- `RL_REQS_PER_MIN = 120` per IP
- `MAX_QUEUE = 10` simultaneous pending sims
- `MAX_MATCHES_PER_DAY = 300` global daily cap (LLM spend ceiling)

### Trust configuration
- `TRUST_XFF` opt-in only. Default trust chain:
  x-real-ip → cf-connecting-ip → fly-client-ip → XFF (only if opt-in).
- BYOK: user-supplied API keys live only in `MATCH_API_KEYS[mid]` for
  ~30 seconds, then popped in the worker's `try/finally`. Never
  persisted, never logged, scrubbed from error surfaces via
  `_KEY_LEAK_RE = re.compile(r"(?i)(bearer|api[_-]?key|token)[=:\s]+\S+|sk-[a-zA-Z0-9_-]{16,}")`.

---

## Security Posture

- **Bandit static analysis:** clean at Medium+ severity (B101, B404,
  B608 skipped with documented justification — assertions in tests,
  subprocess not used in prod paths, and SQL uses parameterized `?`
  placeholders with hardcoded WHERE clauses).
- **Secret scanning:** full git history scanned in CI for `.env`,
  `.key`, `.pem`, real API key patterns (sk-or, sk-ant, AKIA, AIza,
  ghp_). Clean.
- **CSP:** `default-src 'self'`, `script-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'
  'unsafe-eval' https://va.vercel-scripts.com`, `frame-ancestors 'none'`,
  Trusted Types Report-Only.
- **HSTS:** `max-age=63072000; includeSubDomains; preload`
- **COOP:** `same-origin`, **CORP:** `same-origin`
- **Permissions-Policy:** locks down 15 browser APIs (camera, mic,
  geolocation, etc.)
- **CORS:** allowlist (vercel.app + localhost:3000), not `*`.

---

## Honest Limitations (Research Bias Disclosure)

- **Sample size is small.** Elo confidence intervals for individual
  models are wide. Do not cite a specific rating as authoritative.
  Roadmap: bootstrap CIs, provisional K=48 for first 5 votes.
- **Single-annotator voting.** One vote per match = one subjective
  opinion per data point. Inter-rater agreement is not yet measured.
  Roadmap: 3-vote consensus + Krippendorff's α.
- **Prompt is unversioned.** Changing `build_state()` in the backend
  can invalidate historical Elo comparisons. Roadmap: prompt_version
  column in elo table.
- **Self-selected judges.** Users come from Reddit, HN, PeerPush;
  demographic bias is not measured.
- **No calibration set.** No known-answer set of "obviously A won"
  matches to verify user vote accuracy on trivial cases. If accuracy
  is <90% on obvious cases, the entire signal is contaminated.
- **No correlation with established benchmarks.** Reviewer's first
  question: "does top on Stickblade correlate with MMLU / GPQA /
  Arena-Hard?" We haven't run that yet.

---

## Reproducibility

- Replay JSON is stored per match (~5KB/second, gzipped) — every
  match is reproducible from state.
- Match seed persisted via `MATCH_FLIP` dict for A/B canvas assignment.
- Mock brains (used in smoke tests) are NOT currently seeded, so
  mock vs mock matches are non-deterministic. Roadmap:
  `random.seed(int(mid, 16))` in mock init.

---

## Related Work

- **Chatbot Arena (LMSys)** — reference for blind human evaluation
  of LLMs; text-only.
- **OpenAI Evals, HELM** — task-based text evaluation frameworks.
- **Habitat, AI2-THOR** — embodied AI benchmarks; test spatial
  reasoning but in single-agent scripted environments, not
  adversarial multi-agent.
- **Toribash** — physics-based fighting game with real-time joint
  control; used in some robotics research but not for LLM eval.

Stickblade Arena's specific contribution: **adversarial multi-agent
LLM evaluation in a fully-simulated physics environment with blind
human judgment.**

---

## Citation

```
Kumar, A. (2026). Stickblade Arena: A Physics-Based LLM Benchmark
via Blind Adversarial Sword-Fighting.
https://github.com/Cometbuster4969/STICKBLADE-ARENA
```
